text: Neuralink enables those who have lost the ability to speak to speak again https://t.co/Oib1dnctW2
summary: Neuralink enables those who have lost the ability to speak to speak again
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that Neuralink has successfully enabled individuals who lost the ability to speak to communicate again through brain-computer interface technology. This represents a significant product milestone for Neuralink, demonstrating the practical application of neural interface technology beyond theoretical capabilities and validating the company's core value proposition of restoring human function through invasive neural implants.
embed_html:
Neuralink enables those who have lost the ability to speak to speak again https://t.co/Oib1dnctW2

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 10, 2026

text: Starlink provides an Internet connection in flight that is on par with ground Internet speed & latency https://t.co/OyzWLLyyM8
summary: Starlink provides an Internet connection in flight that is on par with ground Internet speed & latency
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Starlink has achieved in-flight internet connectivity with speed and latency comparable to ground-based internet service. This represents a significant product milestone for Starlink's commercial aviation business, demonstrating the viability of satellite internet as a competitive alternative to traditional ground infrastructure for high-speed, low-latency connectivity in previously underserved environments like aircraft.
embed_html:
Starlink provides an Internet connection in flight that is on par with ground Internet speed & latency https://t.co/OyzWLLyyM8

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 10, 2026

text:
First (supervised) FSD approval in Europe!

Congratulations to the Tesla team and thank you to the regulatory authorities in the Netherlands for all the hard work required to make this happen. https://t.co/8hidEOPSxm
summary: First (supervised) FSD approval in Europe! Congratulations to the Tesla team and thank you to the regulatory authorities in the Netherlands for all the hard work required to make this happen.
ai_summary: Tesla achieved its first supervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) approval in Europe, specifically in the Netherlands, following regulatory authorization. This milestone demonstrates Tesla's progress in autonomous driving technology and highlights the importance of working with regulatory bodies to enable deployment of advanced driver assistance systems in new markets—a key business objective for scaling Tesla's software capabilities globally.
embed_html:
First (supervised) FSD approval in Europe!

Congratulations to the Tesla team and thank you to the regulatory authorities in the Netherlands for all the hard work required to make this happen. https://t.co/8hidEOPSxm

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 10, 2026

text:
Tesla is down to the last few hundred Model S & X cars in inventory. 

Poignant end of an era. https://t.co/2ZtIL8Nzmd
summary: Tesla is down to the last few hundred Model S & X cars in inventory. Poignant end of an era.
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Tesla is reducing Model S and X inventory to just a few hundred units, signaling the end of a product era for these legacy models. This reflects Tesla's strategic shift in production priorities, likely toward newer models or manufacturing optimization, and demonstrates how even successful long-running products face eventual phase-out as companies evolve their product portfolios and market positioning.

text: Download the Tesla Robotaxi app https://t.co/x7eVGBWJ1R
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced the launch of the Tesla Robotaxi app, signaling a major milestone in Tesla's autonomous vehicle commercialization strategy. This represents a critical step in Tesla's execution of its self-driving technology roadmap and monetization of its full self-driving (FSD) capabilities through a ride-hailing platform, directly relevant to Tesla's business strategy of diversifying revenue streams beyond vehicle sales into mobility services.
